version.description: version is the manifest schema version (e.g., "1.0"). Used for forward-compatible parsing of manifest files.

metadata.description: metadata contains identifying information about this manifest.

instruments.description: instruments defines the asset types available to this tenant.

accountTypes.description: account_types defines the account classifications available to this tenant.

valuationRules.description: valuation_rules defines how instruments are converted between each other.

sagas.description: sagas defines the workflow definitions available to this tenant.

seedData.description: seed_data contains tenant-specific reference data (e.g., default parties, initial chart of accounts, industry-specific lookups). Uses google.protobuf.Struct for flexible JSONB representation.

paymentRails.description: payment_rails defines external payment provider integrations for this tenant.

partyTypes.description: party_types defines the tenant-configurable party type schemas for this tenant.

mappings.description: mappings defines the bidirectional field mapping definitions available to this tenant. Each mapping describes how an external JSON payload is transformed into an internal Meridian gRPC service call.

operationalGateway.description: operational_gateway defines external system integrations for this tenant.

Manifest.title:
========================================
 Manifest Root
 ========================================
Manifest.description: ======================================== Manifest Root ========================================  Manifest is the atomic configuration snapshot for a Meridian tenant. It declares the complete business model: instruments, account types, valuation rules, and saga workflows. A manifest is self-contained with no external dependencies - all scripts are inline strings and all references resolve within the manifest itself. Codes (instrument codes, account type codes) are immutable primary keys. To change a code, delete the old definition and create a new one.

code.description: code is the unique, immutable identifier for this account type (e.g., "CURRENT", "SAVINGS", "CARBON_INVENTORY"). Must be uppercase alphanumeric with underscores, 1-50 characters.

name.description: name is the human-readable display name (e.g., "Current Account").

normalBalance.title:
========================================
 Account Type Definitions
 ========================================
normalBalance.description: ======================================== Account Type Definitions ========================================  NormalBalance defines the natural balance direction for an account type.

allowedInstruments.description: allowed_instruments lists the instrument codes that this account type can hold. Each value must match a code from the manifest's instruments list. Empty list means all instruments defined in the manifest are allowed.

policies.description: policies contains CEL expressions governing this account type's behavior.

meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypeDef.title: Account Type Definition
meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypeDef.description: AccountTypeDefinition declares an account classification within the manifest. The code field is the immutable primary key - to change a code, delete the old definition and create a new one.

validation.description: validation is a CEL expression evaluated before allowing transactions on this account type. Example: "amount > 0 && amount <= 1000000" Empty string means no additional validation beyond standard checks.

bucketing.description: bucketing is a CEL expression that determines how amounts are grouped into fungibility buckets. Example: "instrument_code + ':' + attributes.batch_id" Empty string means all amounts are fully fungible (single default bucket).

meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypePol.title: Account Type Policies
meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypePol.description: AccountTypePolicies contains CEL expressions that govern account behavior. CEL syntax validation is deferred to the runtime validator - the manifest only stores the expression strings.

headerName.description: header_name is the HTTP header used to transmit the API key. Example: "X-API-Key", "Authorization"

apiKeySecretRef.description: api_key_secret_ref is a reference to the secret store key that holds the API key value. Must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>". Example: "sm://stripe/api_key" or "env://STRIPE_API_KEY"

meridian.control_plane.v1.ApiKeyAuthConf.title: Api Key Auth Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.ApiKeyAuthConf.description: ApiKeyAuthConfig configures authentication via a static API key header. All sensitive values are expressed as secret references, never raw values.

apiKey.description: api_key authenticates using a static API key in a header.

basic.description: basic authenticates using HTTP Basic authentication.

oauth2.description: oauth2 authenticates using OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow.

hmac.description: hmac authenticates by signing requests with HMAC.

mtls.description: mtls authenticates using mutual TLS client certificates.

meridian.control_plane.v1.AuthConfigMani.title: Auth Config Manifest
meridian.control_plane.v1.AuthConfigMani.description: AuthConfigManifest defines the authentication configuration for a provider connection. Exactly one variant must be set. All sensitive values use secret references.

username.description: username is the Basic auth username (not sensitive - stored directly).

passwordSecretRef.description: password_secret_ref is a reference to the secret store key that holds the password. Must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>".

meridian.control_plane.v1.BasicAuthConfi.title: Basic Auth Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.BasicAuthConfi.description: BasicAuthConfig configures HTTP Basic authentication. All sensitive values are expressed as secret references, never raw values.

algorithm.description: algorithm is the HMAC signing algorithm. Supported values: "sha256", "sha512"

secretRef.description: secret_ref is a reference to the secret store key that holds the HMAC signing secret. Must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>".

signatureHeader.description: signature_header is the HTTP header where the computed signature is placed. Example: "X-Signature", "X-Hub-Signature-256"

meridian.control_plane.v1.HMACAuthConfig.title: HMAC Auth Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.HMACAuthConfig.description: HMACAuthConfig configures HMAC-based request signing. All sensitive values are expressed as secret references, never raw values.

externalType.description: external_type identifies the category of inbound event from the provider. Example: "stripe.payment_intent.succeeded", "modulr.account.credited"

handlerSaga.description: handler_saga is the name of the SagaDefinition to invoke when this event is received. Must match a name in the manifest's sagas list.

mappingId.description: mapping_id is the name of the MappingDefinition applied to transform the inbound event payload before passing it to the handler saga. Must match a name in the manifest's mappings list. If empty, the payload is passed as-is.

meridian.control_plane.v1.InboundRouteCo.title: Inbound Route Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.InboundRouteCo.description: InboundRouteConfig declares how inbound events from external providers are handled. This is a Phase 5 placeholder — configuration is captured in the manifest but runtime support is not yet implemented.

instructionType.description: instruction_type is the instruction category this route handles. Must be unique within the manifest's instruction_routes list. Example: "payment.initiate", "account.freeze", "kyc.verify"

connectionId.description: connection_id references the primary ProviderConnectionConfig used to dispatch matching instructions. Must match a connection_id defined in operational_gateway.provider_connections.

fallbackConnectionId.description: fallback_connection_id references an optional secondary connection used when the primary is unhealthy. Must match a connection_id defined in operational_gateway.provider_connections, if set. If not set, instructions fail without fallback when the primary connection is unhealthy.

outboundMappingId.description: outbound_mapping_id is the name of the MappingDefinition applied to transform the instruction payload from the internal Meridian format to the provider's expected format before dispatch. Must match a name in the manifest's mappings list. If empty, the payload is sent as-is.

inboundMappingId.description: inbound_mapping_id is the name of the MappingDefinition applied to transform provider callback payloads from the provider's format to the internal Meridian format. Must match a name in the manifest's mappings list. If empty, callback payloads are processed as-is.

httpMethod.description: http_method is the HTTP method used when dispatching via HTTPS or WEBHOOK protocol. Example: "POST", "PUT", "PATCH" Required for HTTPS and WEBHOOK protocols; ignored for GRPC, MQTT, AMQP.

pathTemplate.description: path_template is the URL path template appended to the connection base_url. Supports simple variable substitution using {field_name} placeholders resolved from the payload. Example: "/v1/payments/{payment_id}/execute", "/accounts/{account_id}/freeze"

meridian.control_plane.v1.InstructionRou.title: Instruction Route Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.InstructionRou.description: InstructionRouteConfig maps an instruction_type to a provider connection for outbound dispatch. Routes determine which connection handles a given type of instruction and how the payload is transformed before dispatch.

code.description: code is the unique, immutable identifier for this instrument (e.g., "GBP", "KWH", "CARBON_CREDIT"). Must be uppercase alphanumeric with underscores, 1-50 characters.

name.description: name is the human-readable display name (e.g., "British Pound Sterling").

type.title:
========================================
 Instrument Definitions
 ========================================
type.description: ======================================== Instrument Definitions ========================================  InstrumentType classifies the broad category of an instrument.

dimensions.description: dimensions describes the unit and precision of this instrument's quantities.

meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDefi.title: Instrument Definition
meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDefi.description: InstrumentDefinition declares an asset type within the manifest. The code field is the immutable primary key - to change a code, delete the old definition and create a new one.

unit.description: unit is the display unit for this instrument (e.g., "GBP", "kWh", "TONNE_CO2E").

precision.description: precision is the number of decimal places for this instrument (0-18).

meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDime.title: Instrument Dimensions
meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDime.description: InstrumentDimensions describes the unit and precision of an instrument's quantities.

clientCertSecretRef.description: client_cert_secret_ref is a reference to the secret store key holding the PEM-encoded client certificate. Must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>".

clientKeySecretRef.description: client_key_secret_ref is a reference to the secret store key holding the PEM-encoded private key. Must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>".

caCertSecretRef.description: ca_cert_secret_ref is an optional reference to the secret store key holding the CA certificate for verifying the provider's server certificate. If set, must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>".

meridian.control_plane.v1.MTLSAuthConfig.title: MTLS Auth Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.MTLSAuthConfig.description: MTLSAuthConfig configures mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication. All sensitive values are expressed as secret references, never raw values.

name.description: name is the human-readable name for this manifest (e.g., "Acme Energy Trading").

industry.description: industry describes the target industry vertical (e.g., "energy", "banking", "carbon").

description.description: description provides additional context about this manifest's purpose.

meridian.control_plane.v1.ManifestMetada.title:
========================================
 Manifest Metadata
 ========================================
meridian.control_plane.v1.ManifestMetada.description: ======================================== Manifest Metadata ========================================  ManifestMetadata contains identifying information about a manifest.

tokenUrl.description: token_url is the OAuth 2.0 token endpoint.

clientId.description: client_id is the OAuth 2.0 client identifier (not sensitive - stored directly).

clientSecretRef.description: client_secret_ref is a reference to the secret store key that holds the client secret. Must use a supported secret URI scheme: "sm://<store>/<key>" or "env://<VAR_NAME>".

scopes.description: scopes lists the OAuth 2.0 scopes to request.

meridian.control_plane.v1.OAuth2AuthConf.title: O Auth 2 Auth Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.OAuth2AuthConf.description: OAuth2AuthConfig configures OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow authentication. All sensitive values are expressed as secret references, never raw values.

providerConnections.description: provider_connections defines the external provider endpoints this tenant connects to.

instructionRoutes.description: instruction_routes maps instruction types to provider connections for outbound dispatch.

inboundRoutes.description: inbound_routes defines how inbound events from external providers are handled (Phase 5 placeholder).

meridian.control_plane.v1.OperationalGat.title:
========================================
 Operational Gateway Config
 ========================================
meridian.control_plane.v1.OperationalGat.description: ======================================== Operational Gateway Config ========================================  OperationalGatewayConfig declares the external system integrations for a tenant. It configures provider connections, outbound instruction routing, and inbound event handling.

mode.title: Connect Mode
mode.description: ConnectMode defines the Stripe Connect account type.

accountId.description: account_id is the Stripe Connect account identifier (format: acct_xxx).

webhookEndpointSecret.description: webhook_endpoint_secret is a reference to the webhook signing secret stored in a secret manager (e.g., "sm://stripe/webhook_secret"). Never contains the plaintext secret.

platformFee.description: platform_fee defines the fee structure for transactions on this rail.

payoutSchedule.title: Payout Schedule
payoutSchedule.description: PayoutSchedule defines when connected account payouts are initiated.

supportedMethods.description: supported_methods lists the payment methods enabled on this rail (e.g., "card", "sepa_debit", "bank_account").

meridian.control_plane.v1.PaymentRails.title: Payment Rails
meridian.control_plane.v1.PaymentRails.description: PaymentRails defines an external payment provider integration. Currently supports Stripe Connect for payment processing.

type.title:
========================================
 Payment Rails
 ========================================
type.description: ======================================== Payment Rails ========================================  PlatformFeeType defines how the platform fee is calculated.

value.description: value is the fee amount as a decimal string. For PERCENTAGE: "2.5" means 2.5%. For FLAT: "0.30" means 0.30 in the transaction currency. Must be a valid positive decimal value.

meridian.control_plane.v1.PlatformFee.title: Platform Fee
meridian.control_plane.v1.PlatformFee.description: PlatformFee defines the fee structure for payment rail transactions.

connectionId.description: connection_id is the stable, unique identifier for this connection within the manifest. Used by InstructionRouteConfig to reference this connection. Must be unique within the manifest. Example: "stripe-payments", "modulr-uk", "kraken-energy-grid"

providerName.description: provider_name is the human-readable name for the external provider. Example: "Stripe", "Modulr", "Kraken Energy Grid"

providerType.description: provider_type is a machine-readable classifier for the provider category. Example: "payment_gateway", "kyc_provider", "energy_scheduler"

protocol.title: Provider Protocol
protocol.description: ProviderProtocol defines the transport protocol used for a provider connection. Mirrors the runtime Protocol enum but is self-contained in the manifest config.

baseUrl.description: base_url is the root URL of the provider's API endpoint. Must be a valid URI. Example: "https://api.stripe.com"

auth.description: auth is the authentication configuration for this connection.

retryPolicy.description: retry_policy defines how failed dispatch attempts are retried. If not set, a default policy is used by the runtime.

rateLimit.description: rate_limit controls the outbound dispatch rate for this connection. If not set, no rate limiting is applied.

meridian.control_plane.v1.ProviderConnec.title: Provider Connection Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.ProviderConnec.description: ProviderConnectionConfig declares a single external provider connection within the manifest. Each connection is identified by a stable connection_id used in InstructionRouteConfig.

requestsPerSecond.description: requests_per_second is the maximum sustained dispatch rate.

burstSize.description: burst_size is the maximum number of requests allowed to exceed the rate limit momentarily.

meridian.control_plane.v1.RateLimitConfi.title: Rate Limit Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.RateLimitConfi.description: RateLimitConfig controls the outbound dispatch rate for a connection.

maxAttempts.description: max_attempts is the total number of dispatch attempts (including the first). A value of 1 means no retries (one attempt only).

initialBackoffSeconds.description: initial_backoff_seconds is the delay before the first retry in seconds.

maxBackoffSeconds.description: max_backoff_seconds caps the computed backoff to prevent unbounded delays.

backoffMultiplier.description: backoff_multiplier is the factor by which the backoff duration grows after each attempt. Example: initial=1s, multiplier=2.0 → 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s...

meridian.control_plane.v1.RetryPolicyCon.title: Retry Policy Config
meridian.control_plane.v1.RetryPolicyCon.description: RetryPolicyConfig defines the backoff strategy for failed dispatch attempts.

name.description: name is the unique identifier for this saga (e.g., "process_energy_settlement"). Must be lowercase alphanumeric with underscores.

trigger.description: trigger defines what initiates this saga. Supported prefixes:   "api:" - triggered by an API call (e.g., "api:/v1/settlements")   "webhook:" - triggered by an external webhook (e.g., "webhook:stripe_payment")   "scheduled:" - triggered on a schedule (e.g., "scheduled:daily_reconciliation")   "event:" - triggered by an internal domain event (e.g., "event:position-keeping.transaction-captured.v1")

script.description: script is the inline Starlark source code defining the saga workflow. Must not be empty - all saga logic is defined inline in the manifest. Starlark guarantees termination (no while loops, no recursion).

filter.description: filter is a CEL expression evaluated against the event payload to determine whether this saga should execute. Recommended when trigger starts with "event:"; omitting it causes a validation warning because the saga will execute for every matching event. The expression must return a boolean; the saga executes only when it returns true. Must be non-empty when present. Example: `event.amount > 0 && event.currency == "GBP"`

meridian.control_plane.v1.SagaDefinition.title:
========================================
 Saga Definitions
 ========================================
meridian.control_plane.v1.SagaDefinition.description: ======================================== Saga Definitions ========================================  SagaDefinition declares a workflow within the manifest. Sagas are triggered by API calls, webhooks, scheduled events, or internal domain events, and contain inline Starlark scripts that orchestrate service interactions.

fromInstrument.description: from_instrument is the source instrument code for conversion. Must match a code from the manifest's instruments list.

toInstrument.description: to_instrument is the target instrument code for conversion. Must match a code from the manifest's instruments list.

method.title:
========================================
 Valuation Rules
 ========================================
method.description: ======================================== Valuation Rules ========================================  ValuationMethod defines how instrument conversion rates are determined.

source.description: source identifies the rate source for this rule. For SPOT_RATE: the feed identifier (e.g., "ecb_fx_daily", "nordpool_spot"). For FIXED: a description of who sets the rate (e.g., "admin_override", "contract_rate").

meridian.control_plane.v1.ValuationRule.title: Valuation Rule
meridian.control_plane.v1.ValuationRule.description: ValuationRule defines how one instrument is converted to another. Used for cross-instrument transactions (e.g., kWh to GBP, USD to EUR).

sourceKeys.description: source_keys lists the gjson paths (external) or proto paths (internal) to collect into the target. Each key's value is placed into the target map under that key name. At least one key must be provided for the flatten to have effect.

targetField.description: target_field is the name of the map field in the target message that receives the flattened values.

meridian.mapping.v1.AttributeFlatten.title: Attribute Flatten
meridian.mapping.v1.AttributeFlatten.description: AttributeFlatten defines a transform that merges multiple source fields into a single target map field. Useful for collecting scattered external fields into an attributes map.

inboundCel.description: inbound_cel is the CEL expression applied when mapping from external to internal format. Example: "value.lowerAscii()" to normalize incoming string values.

outboundCel.description: outbound_cel is the CEL expression applied when mapping from internal to external format. Example: "value.upperAscii()" to format outgoing string values.

meridian.mapping.v1.CelTransform.title: Cel Transform
meridian.mapping.v1.CelTransform.description: CelTransform defines bidirectional CEL expressions for field transformation.

targetPath.description: target_path is the proto field path in the internal message (dot-separated). Example: "created_at", "metadata.ingested_by"

celExpression.description: cel_expression is a CEL expression evaluated against the full source payload. The expression may reference any field from the source document. Example: "now()" for timestamp, "'v1.' + version" for derived strings.

meridian.mapping.v1.ComputedField.title: Computed Field
meridian.mapping.v1.ComputedField.description: ComputedField defines a field whose value is derived from a CEL expression rather than directly mapped from the source payload.

values.description: values maps external enum string values to internal enum string values. Example: {"ACTIVE": "STATUS_ACTIVE", "INACTIVE": "STATUS_INACTIVE"}

fallback.description: fallback is the internal value to use when an external value is not found in the map. If empty, an unmapped inbound value causes an error.

outboundFallback.description: outbound_fallback is the external value to use when an internal value is not found in the map. If empty, an unmapped outbound value causes an error.

meridian.mapping.v1.EnumMapping.title: Enum Mapping
meridian.mapping.v1.EnumMapping.description: EnumMapping defines a bidirectional mapping between external and internal enum values.

externalPath.description: external_path is the gjson path expression for locating the value in the external JSON payload. Supports dot notation, array indexing, and gjson modifiers. Example: "customer.address.city", "items.#.price", "meta|@flatten"

internalPath.description: internal_path is the proto field path in the internal message (dot-separated). Example: "customer_id", "line_items.unit_price", "metadata.source"

transform.description: transform defines an optional value transformation applied during mapping. If not set, the raw string value is used as-is.

meridian.mapping.v1.FieldCorrespondence.title: Field Correspondence
meridian.mapping.v1.FieldCorrespondence.description: FieldCorrespondence maps a single external field to an internal proto field.

cel.description: cel applies bidirectional CEL expressions for value transformation.

enumMapping.description: enum_mapping maps between external and internal enum string representations.

dateFormat.description: date_format specifies a Go time layout string for parsing and formatting date/time values. Example: "2006-01-02" for ISO date, "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00" for RFC3339.

defaultValue.description: default_value is a literal string value used when the source field is absent or empty.

attributeFlatten.description: attribute_flatten collects multiple source keys into a single target map field.

meridian.mapping.v1.FieldTransform.title: Field Transform
meridian.mapping.v1.FieldTransform.description: FieldTransform defines how a single field value is transformed during mapping. At most one transform type may be set (proto oneof semantics). The Go service layer validates that at least one is provided when a FieldTransform is specified.

sourceSelector.description: source_selector is a gjson path expression that extracts the idempotency key from the external payload. Required when use_content_hash is false. Example: "header.idempotency_key", "transaction.reference_id"

useContentHash.description: use_content_hash indicates that the idempotency key should be derived from a hash of the selected content fields rather than a direct field value.

contentHashFields.description: content_hash_fields lists the gjson paths whose values are included in the content hash. Required (non-empty) when use_content_hash is true.

meridian.mapping.v1.IdempotencyConfig.title: Idempotency Config
meridian.mapping.v1.IdempotencyConfig.description: IdempotencyConfig controls how idempotency keys are derived for mapped requests. Cross-field validation (enforced at the Go service layer):   - When use_content_hash is false: source_selector must be non-empty.   - When use_content_hash is true: content_hash_fields must contain at least one entry.

id.description: id is the unique identifier for this mapping definition (UUID).

tenantId.description: tenant_id is the tenant that owns this mapping definition (UUID).

name.description: name is the human-readable name for this mapping. Example: "Stripe Webhook -> Payment Order"

targetService.description: target_service is the Meridian gRPC service this mapping calls. Example: "meridian.payment_order.v1.PaymentOrderService"

targetRpc.description: target_rpc is the RPC method on the target service to invoke after mapping. Example: "InitiatePaymentOrder"

version.description: version is the schema version for this mapping definition (monotonically increasing).

status.title: Mapping Status
status.description: MappingStatus defines the lifecycle state of a mapping definition.

externalSchema.description: external_schema is a JSON Schema string describing the expected external payload structure. Used for documentation and inbound validation.

fields.description: fields defines the field-level correspondences between external and internal formats.

inboundComputedFields.description: inbound_computed_fields defines fields computed during inbound (external -> internal) mapping.

outboundComputedFields.description: outbound_computed_fields defines fields computed during outbound (internal -> external) mapping.

inboundValidationCel.description: inbound_validation_cel is a CEL expression evaluated against the raw external payload before inbound mapping. The expression must return a boolean; false causes rejection. Example: "has(payload.customer_id) && payload.amount > 0"

outboundValidationCel.description: outbound_validation_cel is a CEL expression evaluated against the mapped internal message before outbound mapping. The expression must return a boolean; false causes rejection.

createdAt.description: created_at is when this mapping definition was created.

updatedAt.description: updated_at is when this mapping definition was last modified.

isBatch.description: is_batch indicates this mapping handles array payloads. Cross-field validation (enforced at Go service layer): when is_batch is true, batch_target_path must be non-empty.

batchTargetPath.description: batch_target_path is the gjson path expression that selects the array of items to process when is_batch is true. Required when is_batch is true. Example: "events", "data.items"

idempotency.description: idempotency defines how idempotency keys are derived for requests produced by this mapping.

meridian.mapping.v1.MappingDefinition.title: Mapping Definition
meridian.mapping.v1.MappingDefinition.description: MappingDefinition defines a bidirectional transformation between an external JSON schema and an internal Meridian gRPC service RPC. It supports:   - Field-level correspondence with optional transforms (CEL, enums, date formats)   - Computed fields derived from CEL expressions   - Inbound and outbound CEL validation   - Batch ingestion via gjson array paths   - Idempotency key configuration

id.description: id is the unique identifier for this party type definition.

tenantId.description: tenant_id is the tenant that owns this party type definition.

partyType.description: party_type is the party classification this definition applies to (e.g., "PERSON", "ORGANIZATION"). Uppercase alphanumeric with underscores, max 100 characters.

attributeSchema.description: attribute_schema is a JSON Schema document defining the structure and constraints for party attributes of this type (max 16KB). Must be non-empty when provided.

validationCel.description: validation_cel is a CEL expression for cross-field validation of party attributes. Evaluated at party registration and update time.

eligibilityCel.description: eligibility_cel is a CEL expression for determining account type eligibility for parties of this type.

errorMessageCel.description: error_message_cel is a CEL expression for generating custom error messages when validation or eligibility checks fail.

createdAt.description: created_at is when this party type definition was created.

updatedAt.description: updated_at is when this party type definition was last modified.

meridian.party.v1.PartyTypeDefinition.title: --- Party Type Definitions ---
meridian.party.v1.PartyTypeDefinition.description: --- Party Type Definitions ---  PartyTypeDefinition defines a tenant-configurable schema for a party type, including JSON Schema for attribute validation and CEL expressions for cross-field validation, account eligibility, and custom error messages.
